headline: Apple (AAPL) Faces Italy DMA Probe Over Cloud Interoperability
summary: Italy’s competition authority has opened a Digital Markets Act probe into Apple’s cloud service interoperability on iOS and iPadOS. Regulators are examining whether Apple limits third party consumer cloud providers from using certain system features, potentially favoring iCloud. This is the first investigation launched by a national authority under the EU’s Digital Markets Act and focuses on how Apple’s ecosystem interacts with external services. Apple (NasdaqGS:AAPL) faces this new...

headline: Tata says India pollution board drops scrutiny of Apple iPhone parts plant
summary: Apple's Indian supplier Tata Electronics on Tuesday said a state pollution control board has dropped its scrutiny ‌of the company's iPhone components plant after it addressed concerns about contamination.  The southern Tamil ‌Nadu state's pollution control authority had warned Tata of a forced shutdown unless it explained why government inspections found that ​wastewater discharge had contaminated open wells in adjacent agricultural lands, Reuters reported on Saturday.  On Tuesday, Tata told Reuters in a statement that the Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board had confirmed that the company "has satisfactorily addressed all queries mentioned" in the warning notice and "dropped any further course of action on this ‌issue".

raw.headline: Tata says India pollution board drops scrutiny of Apple iPhone parts plant
raw.summary: Apple's Indian supplier Tata Electronics on Tuesday said a state pollution control board has dropped its scrutiny ‌of the company's iPhone components plant after it addressed concerns about contamination.  The southern Tamil ‌Nadu state's pollution control authority had warned Tata of a forced shutdown unless it explained why government inspections found that ​wastewater discharge had contaminated open wells in adjacent agricultural lands, Reuters reported on Saturday.  On Tuesday, Tata told Reuters in a statement that the Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board had confirmed that the company "has satisfactorily addressed all queries mentioned" in the warning notice and "dropped any further course of action on this ‌issue".

headline: SpaceX Options Start Trading Today. It Will Be Wild.
summary: Options on  SpaceX  stock are scheduled to begin trading when the stock market opens on Tuesday.  The options trading is likely to boost the stock, though it could create some intraday price swings.  Demand for the stock is so high that the options are likely to be among the most actively traded in the entire options market, joining a pedigreed club that includes  Apple Amazon  com,  Nvidia Meta  Platforms, and others in an exclusive club.

headline: Italy's antitrust regulator probes Apple over cloud services under Digital Market rules
summary: Italy's competition regulator said on Tuesday it had ‌opened an investigation into Apple over ‌compliance with interoperability obligations, under the European Digital ​Markets Act.  Under the rules, Apple must ensure that third-party providers of consumer cloud services can inter-operate effectively and free of ‌charge with hardware ⁠and software components controlled through the group's iOS and iPadOS ⁠operating systems, and have equal access as Apple's iCloud service.

headline: T-Mobile warns customers that a key service will double in price
summary: T-Mobile has been rolling out price increases over the past few months and will soon make another despite elevated customer losses. In January, the carrier raised its Regulatory Programs & Telco Recovery fee, which customers pay on their monthly bills. It also started charging $3 a month for ...

headline: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon on AI devices, smart glasses and the future of smartphones
summary: CNBC’s Arjun Kharpal speaks to Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon about the future of AI-powered devices, from smartphones and laptops to smart glasses, pins and other wearable technology. Amon explains how AI agents could change the way people interact with their devices, why apps may evolve rather than disappear and how chips will need to adapt for the next era of computing.
